sentence_reviews.text: Bayesian networks, Markov random fields, and factor graphs factorize a joint distribution into local relationships that support modular probabilistic inference.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Koller & Friedman probabilistic graphical model material; AIMA probabilistic reasoning / Bayesian networks; PML graphical models and factor graphs topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The local factorization and inference characterization is directly aligned with probabilistic graphical model sources.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs is to connect Graph theory, Bayesian & approximate inference to downstream capabilities including Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Koller & Friedman graphical models; AIMA Bayesian networks/probabilistic inference; PML graphical model topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Graph and probabilistic-inference dependencies are supported; the downstream knowledge-graph/semantic-search category is not directly established by these packet sources.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Koller & Friedman relational/probabilistic graphical model context; AIMA probabilistic reasoning and knowledge representation topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relational reasoning is plausible, but semantic search and knowledge graphs as downstream capabilities are not directly supported by the packet evidence.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Koller & Friedman probabilistic graphical models; AIMA Bayesian networks; PML graphical model topics.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Graph theory, Bayesian & approximate inference.

sentence_reviews.notes: Dependencies are supported, but generic limitation wording is not directly evidenced.

sentence_reviews.text: Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Graph theory.

sentence_reviews.notes: Graph structure is central to these models.

sentence_reviews.text: Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Bayesian & approximate inference.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Koller & Friedman inference in graphical models; AIMA probabilistic inference; PML exact/approximate inference in graphical models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Probabilistic inference is a defining use of these models.

sentence_reviews.text: Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Koller & Friedman message passing, EM/learning, and approximate inference; PML graphical model inference and MCMC/EM topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Message passing and approximate inference are standard; MCMC/EM apply in many but not all cases, so common strength is appropriate.
